item.title: Meiji Yasuda Doubles 2026 Super-Long Government Bond Buying Plan
item.description: Meiji Yasuda Life Insurance Co. aims to buy more than ¥2 trillion ($12 billion) of super-long Japanese government bonds in the fiscal year ending March 2027, doubling its earlier purchase plan on the view that yields have climbed to desirable levels.

item.title: China Seen Lifting Some Fuel Export Curbs on Ample Supplies
item.description: China has lifted some restrictions on oil-product exports in the past week, rolling back measures introduced to safeguard domestic supplies shortly after the war began in the Middle East.

item.title: Philippines Sets Record Pay Hike for Manila Workers on Inflation
item.description: Workers in the Philippine capital will see their wages rise by a record 12%, with implementation set to start this month, Labor Secretary Francis Tolentino said on Tuesday.

item.title: SNB Intervened to Halt Rush for Franc at Iran War Outbreak
item.description: The Swiss National Bank sold francs in the first quarter, intervening to stop the currency’s surge when the US and Israel started to attack Iran.

item.title: Shell Sees LNG Trade Flat in 2026 as Hormuz Chokes Supply
item.description: Shell Plc expects global liquefied natural gas supply to be flat year-on-year — after more than a decade of growth — as the conflict in the Middle East chokes supply.

item.title: French Inflation Eases to Lowest Since March as Oil Prices Drop
item.description: French inflation slowed for the first time since the Iran war broke out as energy costs fell, alleviating pressure on the European Central Bank to keep raising interest rates.

item.title: India Plans to Add Strategic Fuel Reserves After Iran War Shock
item.description: India plans to build strategic reserves of crude, liquefied petroleum gas and liquefied natural gas large enough to meet as much as a month of domestic demand, as the country seeks to avoid a repeat of the supply shock created by the war in the Persian Gulf.

item.title: ECB’s Lane Sees Second-Round Effects Taking Time to Appear
item.description: European Central Bank Chief Economist Philip Lane said knock-on effects from higher energy prices will take a while to show up and that policymakers won’t lock themselves into a path for interest rates in the meantime.

item.title: World Bank Approves $750 Million Kenya Loan, Agrees to Back Loan
item.description: The World Bank approved a long-awaited $750 million loan to Kenya in support of reforms aimed at enhancing good governance and improving public-finance management.

item.title: Japan Has Spent Billions to Prop Up the Yen. Why Isn’t It Working?
item.description: The yen’s weakness has become a growing issue for Japan’s policymakers, given its role in driving up import prices and household living costs.

item.title: Indian Farmers Await Early-July Rains to Catch Up on Planting
item.description: Ample rain forecast for early July could help India’s farmers make up for a weak start to the monsoon as the busiest sowing period for key crops approaches.

item.title: Thailand Looks to Invest in US LNG Projects to Secure Supply
item.description: Thailand is considering investments in US liquefied natural gas export projects, according to people familiar with the matter, as the Southeast Asian importer of the fuel looks to shore up its energy security needs.

item.title: UK Households Save Less to Prop Up Spending as Incomes Fall
item.description: Britons put away less in savings in the first quarter as they continued to spend despite a squeeze on living standards from rising prices.

item.title: Japan’s Super-Long Bond Yields Climb as Fiscal Concerns Grow
item.description: Japan’s super-long bond yields rose as Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi’s spending plans deepen concerns about fiscal expansion.

item.title: SpaceX IPO Left Korea Broker With No Shares on Misunderstanding
item.description: It was supposed to be the deal that vaulted Mirae Asset Securities Co. into the global big leagues.

item.title: Softbank-Backed Oravel Updates Filing for $703 Million India IPO
item.description: Oravel Stays Ltd., the parent of travel platform OYO, filed updated paperwork for an initial public offering to raise as much as 66.5 billion rupees ($703 million), reviving one of India’s most closely watched deals.

item.title: UK Economy Grew 0.6% in First Quarter
Biscoff Maker Lotus Gains 590% in Tough Decade for Snack Firms
item.description: Soaring global sales of Biscoff cookies are helping a Belgian food company extend a remarkable decade of stock market outperformance.

item.title: HSBC Says ‘Explosive’ Dollar Rally Is Among Biggest Pain Trades
item.description: A sharp rise in the dollar may emerge as one of the biggest “pain trades” in the second half of the year, according to HSBC Holdings Plc.

item.title: Miners Turn South African Stocks From World-Beaters to Laggards
item.description: The precious-metals miners that propelled South African stocks to a record have become a millstone, dragging the benchmark index toward its worst quarterly slump in more than two years.

item.title: Spain’s War on Football Piracy is Breaking the Internet
item.description: From small businesses to government agencies, legitimate websites are getting caught in the crossfire of LaLiga's fight against illegal sports streams

item.title: Poland’s New Investors Fuel Record Stock Sales for Listed Firms
item.description: Poland’s companies are selling more shares on the secondary market than ever before in a trend driven by increasingly active local investors.

item.title: Growing Oil Glut Spurs Asian Refiners to Offer Cargoes to the US
item.description: Oil output from Persian Gulf nations is ramping up so quickly as the Strait of Hormuz reopens that Asian refiners, already adequately supplied, are pushing some crude to destinations as far afield as California.

item.title: Hormuz Traffic Picks Up as Supertankers Sail Into Persian Gulf
item.description: Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz picked up for the first time since Iran’s recent attacks on ships in the waterway, with more operators sending crude tankers into the Persian Gulf.

item.title: MSC Unit to Invest $1.4 Billion in Adani’s Vizhinjam Port
item.description: A unit of Mediterranean Shipping Co. will invest $1.4 billion to acquire a 49% stake in Adani Vizhinjam Port Pvt., marking one of the largest foreign commitments in India’s port infrastructure.

item.title: Aluminum Set for Worst Monthly Loss Since 2008 on Supply Outlook
item.description: Aluminum headed for the steepest monthly loss since 2008 as expectations for a return of lost Middle Eastern supply saw it rapidly unwind an Iran war-induced rally.

item.title: RBI Funding Curbs Deal ‘Body Blow’ to Indian Prop Trading Firms
item.description: India’s proprietary trading firms and stock brokers are bracing for a major overhaul of how they finance trading activity.

item.title: Trump Lost the Fed Battle, But Look What He Won
item.description: The Supreme Court cleared his right to fire regulators at will — like in markets, crypto.

item.title: Singtel CEO’s Pay Cut 17% to $5.3 Million After Network Outages
item.description: Singapore Telecommunications Ltd. cut Group Chief Executive Officer Yuen Kuan Moon’s pay by 17% after a year marked by emergency service disruption and network outages in Australia and Singapore.

item.title: Japan’s Two-Year Bond Sale Sees Strong Demand on BOJ Caution
item.description: Japan’s two-year government bond auction Tuesday saw stronger demand than the 12-month average, reflecting concerns over the Bank of Japan’s policy path.
